zh.lifeSummary: 當John Sutton IV於1361年12月6日出生在英國瓦維其可爾可爾希爾時，他的父親Sir John Sutton III32歲，母親Katherine Stafford26歲。他於大約1380年與Alice le Despencer結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他於1388年被列為mp for lincoln。他去世於1397年3月10日享年35歲。

nameMeanings.name: Sutton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places called Sutton, named with Old English sūth ‘south, southern’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. The English surname is also common in Ireland (Wexford, Kildare), where it has been established since the 13th century and Gaelicized as de Sutún.
Jewish (from the former Ottoman Empire, including Syria): unexplained.
English: topographic name from Middle English (bi) suthentune ‘(at the place to the) south of the village’.

variants.name: Setton
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Avi, Ronit, Yossef.  French Andree, Henri, Jacques, Maryse.
Jewish (from the Ottoman Empire): altered form of   Sutton  .

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When John Sutton IV was born on 6 December 1361, in Coleshill, Warwickshire, England, his father, Sir John Sutton III, was 32 and his mother, Katherine Stafford, was 26. He married Alice le Despencer about 1380. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. In 1388, at the age of 26, his occupation is listed as mp for lincoln. He died on 10 March 1397, at the age of 35.

Il a épousé Alice le Despencer environ 1380. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. En 1388, à l’âge de 26 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est mp for lincoln. Il est décédé le 10 mars 1397, à l’âge de 35 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als John Sutton IV am 6. Dezember 1361 in Coleshill, Warwickshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir John Sutton III 32 und seine Mutter Katherine Stafford 26. Er hat um 1380 Alice le Despencer geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. 1388, als er 26 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als mp for lincoln aufgeführt. Er starb am 10. März 1397 im Alter von 35 Jahren.
